name: Mancha Real - round trip via Almadén
name_original: Mancha Real – ida y vuelta por Almadén
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Mancha Real are situated in the province of Jaén, Andalusia, at the foothills of the Sierra Mágina mountains. The terrain features significant elevation changes, providing challenging climbs and rewarding descents for cyclists. The region is characterized by extensive olive groves, creating a distinctive visual landscape for cycling. Prominent natural features like the Peña del Águila contribute to the varied topography. Best road cycling routes around Mancha Real The most popular road cycling route is Puerto de Albanchez Loop from Mancha Real, a 30.7 miles (49.5 km) difficult trail that takes 2 hours 45 minutes to complete. This route offers substantial elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Mancha Real - round trip via Almadén, a difficult 29.0 miles (46.6 km) path. This route navigates through the characteristic olive groves of the region, providing scenic views. Local road cyclists also love the Tour of the Sierra Mágina Natural Park, a 78.0 miles (125.5 km) trail leading through the Sierra Mágina Natural Park, often completed in about 6 hours 26 minutes. Road cycling around Mancha Real is defined by challenging climbs in the Sierra Mágina mountains, tranquil stretches through olive groves, and panoramic viewpoints. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Mancha Real are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from more than 4 reviews. More than 700 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Mancha Real's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Mancha Real?
A: Mancha Real offers over 30 road cycling routes, catering to various skill levels. You'll find 3 easy routes, 11 moderate routes, and 20 difficult routes, primarily through the Sierra Mágina mountains and extensive olive groves.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling around Mancha Real?
A: Road cycling around Mancha Real is characterized by significant elevation changes, with challenging climbs and rewarding descents through the Sierra Mágina mountains. You'll also experience tranquil stretches winding through vast olive groves, offering a unique visual landscape.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Mancha Real?
A: The best time for road cycling in Mancha Real is typically during spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). These seasons offer milder temperatures, making the challenging climbs more enjoyable and providing comfortable conditions for longer rides through the olive groves.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or less experienced road cyclists?
A: Yes, Mancha Real offers a few routes suitable for beginners. While the region is known for its challenging terrain, there are 3 easy routes available. These routes generally feature less elevation gain and provide a gentler introduction to road cycling in the area.

Q: Are there challenging routes for advanced road cyclists?
A: Absolutely. Mancha Real is an excellent destination for advanced road cyclists, with 20 difficult routes available. These routes often involve substantial elevation gain and long distances, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996029257/tour-of-the-sierra-magina-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of the Sierra Mágina Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 125.5 km with over 2,100 meters of elevation.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer views of the majestic Sierra Mágina mountains and the prominent Peña del Águila. You can also pass by significant peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4444835" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Mágina Summit&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5312861" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almadén Summit Radio Repeater&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes may also lead you near natural monuments like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4754151" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrance to the Sierra Mágina Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; or the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5391573" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Charca de Pegalajar&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there routes with panoramic views?
A: Yes, road cycling routes in and around Mancha Real, especially those ascending into the Sierra Mágina, frequently offer breathtaking panoramic views. You'll be treated to expansive vistas of the surrounding mountains, valleys, and the iconic 'sea' of olive plantations that define the region.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes in Mancha Real?
A: Many of the road cycling routes in Mancha Real are designed as circular tours, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996092456/puerto-de-albanchez-loop-from-mancha-real" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Albanchez Loop from Mancha Real&lt;/a&gt; and the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40358734" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jaén Cathedral – La Pandera loop from Mancha Real&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Mancha Real?
A: The road cycling routes in Mancha Real are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars. Cyclists often praise the challenging climbs, the stunning panoramic views, and the peaceful experience of riding through the extensive olive groves on quiet roads.

Q: Is parking available for cyclists in Mancha Real?
A: Mancha Real, as a local town, generally offers various public parking options. It's advisable to look for designated parking areas within the town center or near the start points of popular routes. Specific parking details for individual routes are often available on their respective komoot tour pages.

Q: Are there places to eat or stay near the cycling routes?
A: Mancha Real and surrounding towns offer various amenities for cyclists. You can find local cafes and restaurants serving traditional Andalusian cuisine, often featuring the region's renowned extra virgin olive oil. Accommodation options range from guesthouses to hotels, providing a comfortable base for exploring the cycling routes.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling options in Mancha Real?
A: While many routes in Mancha Real are quite challenging due to the mountainous terrain, families looking for gentler rides might find suitable options among the 3 easy routes available. These routes typically have less elevation and are more manageable for a family outing. Always check the route details for specific difficulty and distance.
tips: The church is the most significant building in Mancha Real and the most prestigious architects from the 16th to the 18th centuries have participated in its construction, such as Andrés de Vandelvira, Juan de Aranda and Eufrasio López de Rojas. The church is located in the Plaza de la Constitución.

One of the best climbs in Andalusia. The ascent begins as soon as you leave Mancha Real. The length of the climbs is 8.47 km with a positive drop of 793 m with an average gradient of 7.4%, so it is expected to be hard and long, reaching 2,012 m. Climb is used during the TransIberica race.

waypoints.name: Parish Church of San Juan Evangelista, Mancha Real
